While high-level relationship management and complex negotiation (like M&A) provide some insulation, the core tasks of identifying investment opportunities, executing trades, and providing routine financial advice are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains that could reduce the need for human agents.
robotics_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely cognitive and social, centered on financial analysis, advisory services, and relationship management. Since the work is performed in office environments or through digital platforms, there are virtually no physical tasks for humanoid robots or autonomous systems to automate.

title: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers

title: Semiconductor processing technicians

title: Set and exhibit designers

title: Sheet metal workers

title: Skincare specialists

title: Small engine mechanics

title: Social and community service managers

robotics_rationale: This occupation is primarily cognitive and social, focusing on program management, community outreach, and personnel supervision. title: Social and human service assistants

While it involves some physical movement and site visits, the core duties require high-level human judgment and interpersonal trust that are currently beyond the capabilities of robotics.

title: Social workers

robotics_rationale: Social work is fundamentally centered on empathy, complex interpersonal communication, and nuanced crisis intervention, which are highly resistant to physical automation. title: Sociologists

robotics_rationale: Sociology is a highly cognitive and social profession focused on analyzing complex human behaviors, social structures, and policy. The role involves no significant manual labor or repetitive physical tasks, as the work is primarily conducted in office settings or through interpersonal field research, making it largely immune to physical automation.

title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers

robotics_rationale: Software development and testing are purely cognitive and digital occupations with no physical labor requirements. Humanoid robots and physical automation target manual tasks, whereas these professionals are the ones who actually design and program such systems.

title: Solar photovoltaic installers

title: Special education teachers

robotics_rationale: Special education is primarily centered on complex social interaction, emotional intelligence, and highly individualized educational planning, which are significant barriers to robotics. title: Special effects artists and animators

title: Speech-language pathologists
exposure_rationale: Speech-language pathology involves a significant amount of digital-adjacent work, such as analyzing speech patterns, documenting progress, and developing treatment plans, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for swallowing therapy, real-time interpersonal coaching, and high-stakes clinical judgment that limits full automation.
robotics_rationale: Speech-language pathology is primarily a cognitive and social occupation requiring high levels of empathy, complex diagnostic reasoning, and personalized treatment planning. While it involves some physical components like swallowing therapy, these tasks require delicate human touch and real-time adjustments that are difficult for robotics to replicate.

title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical maintenance, manual repairs, and hands-on troubleshooting of heavy mechanical equipment in unpredictable physical environments. While AI and automated control systems can enhance monitoring and data logging, they cannot perform the physical tasks of overhauling valves, lubricating parts, or crawling inside boilers for inspections.
robotics_rationale: Much of the monitoring and control functions are already being digitized through sensors and automated control systems. While complex mechanical repairs and troubleshooting in confined spaces like boiler interiors remain challenging for robots, the structured nature of industrial plants makes the routine inspection and maintenance tasks highly susceptible to future humanoid and specialized robotics.

title: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors
exposure_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-level knowledge work and intense interpersonal interaction. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like documentation, treatment planning, and monitoring progress, the core of the job requires human empathy, crisis intervention, and the navigation of complex emotional and physical environments that AI cannot yet replicate.
robotics_rationale: This occupation is centered on complex social interaction, emotional intelligence, and human empathy, which are significant barriers to robotics. There are virtually no repetitive manual tasks that a robot could perform to replace or significantly reshape the core functions of a counselor.

title: Surgical assistants and technologists
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, sterile technique, and real-time presence in an operating room. While AI and robotics can assist with surgical precision or inventory management, the role remains centered on hands-on patient care and physical equipment handling that cannot be digitized.
robotics_rationale: Many core tasks, such as sterilizing equipment, counting supplies, and holding retractors, are repetitive and occur in highly structured environments, making them targets for automation. However, the need for high-stakes human judgment, real-time communication with the surgical team, and the dexterity required to assist in unpredictable medical emergencies provide a significant barrier to full robotic replacement.

title: Survey researchers
exposure_rationale: Survey research is a fundamentally digital knowledge occupation involving background research, survey design, statistical analysis, and report generation—all areas where LLMs and specialized AI tools excel. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to data mining and big data efficiencies, and the rapid advancement of AI in synthesizing qualitative data and automating complex statistical workflows makes this role highly susceptible to significant restructuring and labor reduction.
robotics_rationale: Survey research is a highly cognitive and social occupation centered on data analysis, research design, and human interaction. The role lacks repetitive physical tasks, making it virtually immune to displacement by humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Surveying and mapping technicians
exposure_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS software) significantly enhance data analysis, map creation, and calculation tasks, the role still requires a physical presence to navigate rugged terrain, set markers, and operate equipment in the real world.
robotics_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical fieldwork in semi-structured environments where robotics, particularly drones and robotic total stations, are already standard tools. While navigating rugged terrain and interpreting complex spatial data require human judgment, the core tasks of data collection and site measurement are highly susceptible to further automation by autonomous mobile robots and aerial systems.

title: Surveyors
exposure_rationale: Surveying is a hybrid occupation that combines significant physical fieldwork with digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS) are rapidly increasing productivity in the mapping and analysis phases, the core requirements of physical site visits, navigating rugged terrain, and providing legally certified human testimony in court create a substantial barrier to full automation.
robotics_rationale: While drones and robotic total stations have already automated significant portions of physical data collection and measurement, the occupation remains anchored by complex legal research and professional certification requirements. The highly unstructured and varied outdoor environments of field sites present challenges for full physical automation, and human judgment is essential for reconciling historical property discrepancies and providing expert testimony.

title: Tax examiners and collectors, and revenue agents
exposure_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—reviewing financial documents, identifying discrepancies, and interpreting tax law—are digital information-processing tasks where AI excels. While revenue agents handling complex corporate audits and collectors performing field investigations require human judgment and interpersonal skills, a significant portion of the routine examination and data verification work is already being automated or enhanced by sophisticated algorithms.
robotics_rationale: This occupation is primarily cognitive and digital, focusing on financial analysis, legal interpretation, and interpersonal communication. While field audits involve some physical movement to different locations, the core duties lack repetitive manual tasks that would benefit from humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Taxi drivers, shuttle drivers, and chauffeurs
exposure_rationale: While the core task of driving is highly susceptible to automation through autonomous vehicle technology (AI), the job currently requires significant physical presence for tasks like loading luggage, assisting passengers with disabilities, and performing vehicle maintenance. The transition to fully driverless fleets faces regulatory and technical hurdles in complex environments, but the long-term trajectory for this digital-adjacent physical task is one of high potential displacement.
robotics_rationale: The core task of operating a vehicle is the primary target of autonomous driving technology, which is already being deployed in various urban environments. While secondary duties like providing physical assistance to passengers or acting as a personal assistant offer some protection, the majority of the role's functions are highly vulnerable to displacement by autonomous systems.

title: Teacher assistants
exposure_rationale: Teacher assistants have moderate exposure because AI can significantly automate their administrative and instructional support tasks, such as grading, lesson preparation, and providing personalized tutoring through AI agents. However, a large portion of the role involves physical supervision, behavioral management, and assisting students with special needs or personal hygiene, which require a physical human presence that AI cannot replace.
robotics_rationale: Teacher assistants perform a blend of instructional support and physical care in highly dynamic environments with unpredictable children. While robotics could assist with minor tasks like material preparation or basic monitoring, the core requirements of behavioral management, emotional support, and sensitive physical assistance for special needs students remain difficult to automate.

title: Technical writers
exposure_rationale: Technical writing is a fully digital occupation centered on synthesizing complex information into clear documentation, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already draft manuals, summarize technical specs, and generate code documentation, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human writers needed for routine documentation tasks.
robotics_rationale: Technical writing is a purely cognitive and digital occupation that involves synthesizing complex information into documentation. It lacks any significant physical or manual components that would be susceptible to humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Telecommunications technicians
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical labor, including climbing towers, installing hardware, and manual troubleshooting in unpredictable environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI can assist with remote diagnostics, network optimization, and predictive maintenance, the physical necessity of splicing cables and mounting equipment ensures the role remains primarily human-driven.
robotics_rationale: Telecommunications technicians perform significant manual labor in semi-structured environments where automation, such as drones for tower inspections and remote diagnostic software, is already making inroads. While the high dexterity required for tasks like fiber splicing and the need to navigate unpredictable spaces like attics or crawl spaces provide a barrier to full automation, the overall demand for manual intervention is declining as infrastructure becomes more self-monitoring and resilient.

title: Tellers
exposure_rationale: Tellers face high exposure because their core tasks—processing routine financial transactions and verifying information—are digital in nature and highly susceptible to automation through AI and advanced self-service technology. While the physical handling of cash provides a slight buffer, the rapid shift toward mobile banking, AI-driven fraud detection, and interactive video kiosks is significantly reducing the need for human intervention in these roles.
robotics_rationale: Tellers perform highly repetitive physical tasks, such as counting and dispensing cash, in a structured environment that is already being transformed by physical automation. While some customer service and advisory components remain, the core manual duties are increasingly handled by enhanced ATMs and automated kiosks, leading to a significant decline in the need for human intervention.

title: Top executives
exposure_rationale: Top executives perform high-level knowledge work including financial analysis, strategic planning, and contract review, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the role involves human-centric responsibilities like leadership, high-stakes negotiation, and accountability to boards or the public, which provide a significant buffer against full automation.
robotics_rationale: Top executive roles are almost entirely cognitive and social, centered on strategic decision-making, leadership, and complex negotiations. There are no repetitive physical tasks in structured environments that would be susceptible to humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Tour and travel guides
exposure_rationale: While AI can automate the digital aspects of the job—such as researching history, planning itineraries, and providing multilingual translations—the core of the role requires physical presence and real-time human interaction. Guides must physically escort groups through unpredictable environments, manage group dynamics, and provide a personal, empathetic experience that digital tools cannot fully replicate.
robotics_rationale: While robots can navigate structured environments like museums and provide recorded information, the core of this role involves high-level social interaction, storytelling, and adapting to unpredictable group dynamics. The physical tasks of escorting and monitoring are secondary to the complex interpersonal and cognitive skills that remain difficult to automate.

title: Training and development managers
exposure_rationale: This role is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the creation of instructional content, data-driven needs assessments, and budget management—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human-centric leadership and stakeholder collaboration, AI can automate the generation of training materials, personalize learning paths, and analyze program effectiveness, substantially increasing the productivity of each manager.
robotics_rationale: Training and development managers perform purely cognitive and social work, focusing on organizational strategy, personnel management, and program evaluation. Their duties are conducted in office environments and involve complex human interactions that lack the repetitive physical components targeted by humanoid robotics.

title: Training and development specialists
exposure_rationale: Much of the core work, such as designing training manuals, creating online learning modules, and assessing needs through data, is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal skills for live delivery and collaboration, AI can now generate curriculum, simulate training scenarios, and personalize learning paths, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of specialists needed for content creation.
robotics_rationale: Training and development specialists perform primarily cognitive and interpersonal work, such as curriculum design, needs assessment, and social facilitation. The role lacks repetitive manual tasks or physical labor in structured environments, making it highly resistant to displacement by humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers
exposure_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as logistics planning, budgeting, and supply chain optimization, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains. However, the core job also requires physical presence for facility inspections, safety compliance monitoring, and high-stakes interpersonal leadership in unpredictable environments like warehouses and railyards, providing a buffer against full automation.
robotics_rationale: While these managers oversee environments heavily impacted by robotics, such as warehouses and logistics hubs, their own duties are primarily cognitive and involve strategic planning, budgeting, and personnel management. Robotics will likely change the nature of the operations they supervise, but the core managerial functions of problem-solving and cross-departmental coordination remain difficult to automate.

title: Travel agents
exposure_rationale: The core functions of a travel agent—researching destinations, comparing prices, building itineraries, and processing bookings—are entirely digital and involve information synthesis that AI can now perform instantly. While high-end human agents provide personalized judgment and crisis management, AI's ability to handle complex natural language queries and real-time logistics makes the vast majority of the occupation's routine tasks highly susceptible to automation.
robotics_rationale: Travel agents perform almost exclusively cognitive and digital tasks, such as researching, booking, and communicating with clients via computer and phone. The role lacks repetitive manual labor or physical tasks in structured environments that would be susceptible to humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Tutors
exposure_rationale: Tutors primarily engage in knowledge transfer, explanation, and feedback—tasks where generative AI and personalized learning algorithms excel. While the interpersonal and motivational aspects of tutoring provide some protection, the core work product is digital/informational, and AI's ability to provide 24/7, low-cost, personalized instruction for standardized tests and core subjects creates significant displacement risk.
robotics_rationale: Tutoring is primarily a cognitive and social occupation centered on knowledge transfer and interpersonal interaction, which provides a natural barrier to physical automation. While AI software may automate the digital aspects of the role, the physical components are minimal and lack the repetitive manual nature typical of high-exposure jobs.

title: Umpires, referees, and other sports officials
exposure_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and objective data processing. While computer vision and AI can already automate 'calls' (like strike zones or out-of-bounds) with higher accuracy than humans, the role still requires physical presence to manage player conduct, ensure safety, and handle real-time interpersonal disputes on the field.
robotics_rationale: While computer vision and sensor technologies are increasingly automating objective tasks like line calls and ball-strike counts, the role remains heavily dependent on complex human judgment and split-second decision-making. Furthermore, the social requirement of managing player emotions, settling disputes, and interpreting intent in dynamic environments provides a significant barrier to full robotic replacement.

title: Urban and regional planners
exposure_rationale: Urban planners perform heavy knowledge work including data analysis, GIS mapping, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. However, the role requires significant physical site inspections and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with government officials and the public, which provides a buffer against total automation.
robotics_rationale: Urban and regional planning is primarily a cognitive and social occupation requiring complex data analysis, policy interpretation, and stakeholder negotiation. While drones may assist with site inspections and aerial data collection, the core duties are non-routine and rely on human judgment and interpersonal skills that are not susceptible to physical automation.

title: Veterinarians
exposure_rationale: Veterinary work is a hybrid of physical procedures and complex diagnostic knowledge work. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through image analysis (X-rays/ultrasounds) and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical surgery, manual examinations, and high-stakes interpersonal communication with pet owners that cannot be automated.
robotics_rationale: Veterinarians perform complex, non-routine physical tasks such as surgery and medical examinations that require high manual dexterity and the ability to manage unpredictable animal behavior. While robotics may assist in precision surgery or diagnostics, the core duties require high-level medical judgment and compassionate communication with owners that are difficult to automate.

title: Veterinary assistants and laboratory animal caretakers
exposure_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and manual, involving the handling, feeding, and restraining of live animals in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or diagnostic data entry, it cannot replace the physical presence and manual dexterity required for animal care, sterilization, and surgical assistance.
robotics_rationale: Many routine tasks such as cleaning cages, disinfecting facilities, and dispensing food are highly susceptible to automation. However, the core duties of restraining unpredictable animals, providing compassionate care, and assisting in medical procedures require high manual dexterity and emotional intelligence that remain difficult for robotics to replicate.

title: Veterinary technologists and technicians
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks that AI cannot perform, such as restraining animals, administering anesthesia, performing surgery prep, and providing emergency first aid. However, AI has significant exposure in the diagnostic and administrative aspects of the job, including analyzing lab results, interpreting X-rays, and managing patient records, which may increase individual productivity.
robotics_rationale: While laboratory diagnostics and data recording are increasingly automated, the core duties involve handling unpredictable, non-cooperative animals and performing delicate medical procedures that require high manual dexterity. The need for human judgment in emergency care and emotional intelligence when communicating with pet owners creates a significant barrier to full robotic replacement.

title: Waiters and waitresses
exposure_rationale: While AI and automation are increasingly handling digital tasks like order-taking and payments via kiosks or mobile apps, the core of the job remains physical and interpersonal. The physical requirements of carrying trays, clearing tables, and providing real-time human hospitality in a dynamic environment create a significant barrier to full AI replacement.
robotics_rationale: A significant portion of this role involves repetitive physical tasks such as transporting food, clearing tables, and stocking service areas, which are already being addressed by autonomous mobile robots. While high-end hospitality requires complex social interaction, the majority of the workforce in casual dining is highly exposed to automation through self-service kiosks and robotic food runners.

title: Water and wastewater treatment plant and system operators
exposure_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical labor and digital monitoring, with AI and advanced automation already contributing to a projected 7% decline in employment. While AI can optimize chemical dosing, analyze sensor data, and manage complex system flows, the role still requires physical presence for equipment maintenance, manual sampling, and emergency response to mechanical failures.
robotics_rationale: The BLS explicitly projects a decline in this occupation due to increasing automation of treatment processes and monitoring systems. While human oversight is still required for emergency response and complex maintenance, routine tasks like chemical adjustment, data recording, and equipment inspection are highly susceptible to robotic and sensor-based automation.

title: Water transportation workers
exposure_rationale: The core of the work is physical and requires real-time presence in unpredictable maritime environments, involving manual tasks like equipment maintenance, cargo handling, and emergency response. While AI can assist with navigation, fuel optimization, and predictive maintenance for engineers, the high-stakes physical safety requirements and the need for human intervention in complex docking or mechanical failures limit overall exposure.
robotics_rationale: Autonomous vessel technology is significantly advancing in navigation and engine monitoring, which directly impacts the roles of captains, pilots, and engineers. While routine maintenance and deck work are susceptible to robotics, the unpredictable nature of marine environments and the necessity for human intervention during emergencies and complex docking maneuvers maintain a need for human oversight.

title: Web developers and digital designers
exposure_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, with core tasks like writing code (HTML, JavaScript), designing layouts, and creating prototypes being areas where AI has demonstrated high proficiency. AI tools can now generate functional code from natural language and create sophisticated visual mockups, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of developers needed for standard projects.
robotics_rationale: Web development and digital design are purely cognitive and digital occupations with no physical labor requirements. Since the work is performed entirely on computers and involves no manual tasks in physical environments, it has no exposure to humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Welders, cutters, solderers, and brazers
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, physical stamina, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable environments like construction sites or repair shops. While AI and robotics can automate routine assembly-line welding, the specialized, non-routine tasks and the need for physical presence in varied locations provide a strong barrier against AI displacement.
robotics_rationale: Welding and soldering are highly repetitive, manual tasks often performed in structured manufacturing environments, which are primary targets for robotic automation. While complex field repairs and custom construction require human judgment, a significant portion of the workforce is already being supplemented or replaced by robotic arms and advanced automated systems.

title: Wholesale and manufacturing sales representatives
exposure_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, communication, and data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While high-stakes B2B negotiations and physical trade shows provide a buffer, AI can now handle lead generation, personalized outreach, price optimization, and technical product explanations, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of representatives needed.
robotics_rationale: The core of this occupation involves complex social interaction, negotiation, and relationship management, which are highly resistant to physical automation. While autonomous vehicles may eventually assist with travel, the primary value of the role lies in human-to-human persuasion and technical consultation in varied, unstructured environments.

title: Wind turbine technicians
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-stakes physical labor, including climbing 200-foot towers, rappelling, and performing manual repairs in confined spaces. While AI can assist with remote monitoring and predictive maintenance diagnostics, the physical requirement to replace components and perform manual inspections in unpredictable outdoor environments provides a strong barrier to automation.
robotics_rationale: Significant portions of the role, particularly blade and tower inspections, are already being automated by drones and specialized climbing robots. While complex mechanical troubleshooting and repairs in confined nacelles still require human dexterity and judgment, the standardized nature of wind turbines makes them a prime target for specialized robotic maintenance systems.

title: Woodworkers
exposure_rationale: The core of the job involves physical manipulation of materials, manual dexterity, and real-time sensory monitoring in a workshop environment, which are resistant to AI. While AI can optimize digital design (CAD) and CNC programming, the physical assembly, finishing, and machine maintenance require a human presence that limits overall exposure.
robotics_rationale: Woodworking involves highly repetitive physical tasks such as feeding machines, sanding, and assembly within structured factory or shop environments. The occupation is already seeing a decline due to CNC automation, and advances in robotics will likely automate remaining manual tasks like material handling and complex assembly.

title: Writers and authors
exposure_rationale: The core work product of writers and authors is entirely digital and text-based, which aligns perfectly with the primary capabilities of Large Language Models. AI can now generate drafts, conduct research, and perform editing tasks across various formats like blogs, copy, and scripts, significantly increasing productivity while simultaneously threatening to automate many entry-level and routine writing roles.
robotics_rationale: Writing and authorship are purely cognitive and digital tasks that involve no manual labor or physical interaction with the environment. Because the work is performed entirely through computers and intellectual processes, it has no exposure to humanoid robotics or physical automation.

title: Zoologists and wildlife biologists
exposure_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. AI significantly impacts the data-heavy aspects of the job, such as analyzing GIS data, processing camera trap imagery, and modeling population dynamics, but it cannot replace the physical requirements of collecting biological specimens, handling wild animals, or navigating remote outdoor environments.
robotics_rationale: While the role involves significant fieldwork and physical activity, these tasks occur in highly unpredictable and unstructured natural environments that are difficult for robotics to navigate. Automation in this field, such as drones and remote sensors, primarily serves as a tool to augment data collection rather than replacing the core scientific analysis, judgment, and complex problem-solving performed by the biologist.
